snippet.title: AI wrote code, but forgot about performance - fix it with 1 prompt | Angel Pichardo, ImageKit.io
snippet.description:
AI built your app. It didn't optimize your images. Here's how 1 MCP prompt takes you from 68MB to 2MB вЂ“ automatically вљЎ

What's inside:
вњ… How Chrome DevTools MCP diagnoses real performance issues in your live app
вњ… How ImageKit MCP reads its own docs and generates the fix without you leaving the editor
вњ… Why image optimization is now a prompt, not a task

рџЋ™ Angel Pichardo is a Senior Developer Advocate at ImageKit.
10+ years in engineering, now building at the intersection of AI agents and developer tooling. His talks skip the theory: he starts with a broken app and fixes it live.

snippet.title: Who reviews your agent's code? | Santosh Yadav, CodeRabbit
snippet.description:
рџ¤– AI writes code faster than you can review it. Here's how engineers are adapting вЂ“ and what comes next рџ‘€

What's inside:
вќ“ CLI, IDE, or agentic loop вЂ” which review style actually works in 2026
вќ“ Why the 18-year-old GitHub diff view is now your biggest bottleneck
вќ“ How to review AI-generated code without losing your mind (or your database)

рџЋ™ Santosh Yadav is a Principal Developer Advocate at CodeRabbit - an AI code review platform trusted by thousands of engineering teams worldwide. He's a Google Developer Expert for Angular, GitHub Star, Nx Champion, and Microsoft MVP. Santosh actively contributes to Angular and its ecosystem, champions monorepo architecture, and has been helping developers ship better code for years.

snippet.title: I Ship a Production App With No Backend вЂ“ This Is the Architecture | Mikkel Malmberg, Tether
snippet.description:
рџ”Ґ What if every new user made your app stronger вЂ“ not your bill bigger? We show how they shipped Keet, a production messenger, with zero centralized backend вЂ“ using cryptographic keys, signed messages, and deterministic state sync instead.

рџ‘‰ This is local-first, P2P architecture that actually runs in production.

What's inside:
вњ… Why your server is just a reducer вЂ” and how to remove it from the equation completely
вњ… How to replace auth, storage, and state sync with cryptography and a deterministic log
вњ… The Peer SDK Tether is building so any developer can ship P2P apps without thinking about the crypto layer

рџЋ™ Mikkel Malmberg is a programmer and indie app builder вЂ“ founder of Brainbow, a tiny product studio building platforms for creators. He's been shipping macOS apps, tools, and side projects since 2007, and recently stumbled into P2P.
Connect with Mikkel:
рџ”— x.com/mikker
Explore what he ships:
рџ”— mikkelmalmberg.com
Learn more about Tether:
рџ”— tether.io

рџ•ђ Timestamps:
00:00 - The problem: more users = bigger bills
01:55 - What if users helped instead of cost you?

snippet.title: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini вЂ“ parallel agents in one editor | Zed live demo | Gaauwe Rombouts
snippet.description:
Run Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini in parallel вЂ“ without leaving your editor. рџ’« Gaauwe Rombouts from Zed shows how agentic workflows actually work in practice, live on stage at React Summit вљЎ

What's inside:
вњ… How to run multiple AI agents simultaneously in isolated work trees вЂ” no context switching, no friction
вњ… Zed's open agent architecture: use any agent (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) through one unified interface
вњ… Live demo: real mistakes, real fixes, real parallel builds вЂ” what actually happens when you let agents loose

рџЋ™ Gaauwe Rombouts is a Frontend Engineer at Zed, building the web experience around a code editor obsessed with speed and AI collaboration. As one of the few TypeScript developers in a Rust-first team, he bridges the gap between web and systems engineering вЂ“ and knows how to keep three AI agents running live on stage without missing a beat.
Explore Gaauwe's work:

snippet.title: 3 Places Firebase Breaks Your Next.js UX (Auth, Firestore, Remote Config) | RosГЎrio Fernandes
snippet.description:
рџ§© Most Firebase flicker bugs aren't bugs. They're architectural mismatches between client SDKs and React Server Components. Here's how to fix all three вЂ“ with FirebaseServerApp, onSnapshotResume, and Remote Config Server SDK.

What's inside:
вљЎ The auth flicker: why your server renders a Sign In button even for logged-in users вЂ” and the fix
вљЎ The Firestore flicker: offline cache + real-time updates = 2 extra renders. How to collapse them into zero
вљЎ The Remote Config flicker: why feature flags flash on load and how server-side fetch eliminates it

рџЋ™ RosГЎrio P. Fernandes is a Developer Relations Engineer on the Firebase team at Google, helping developers build with Firebase and Generative AI across mobile and web. A passionate community builder, he's a regular presence at Android, Firebase, and Flutter developer events worldwide.
Connect with RosГЎrio:
рџ‘‰ https://twitter.com/thatfiredev
рџ‘‰ https://linkedin.com/in/rosariopfernandes
рџ‘‰ https://github.com/thatfiredev
рџ‘‰ https://bsky.app/profile/thatfire.dev
Firebase:
рџ‘‰ https://firebase.google.com/

рџ“ќ Talk: Taming the Flicker: Firebase Patterns for React Server Components | Rosario Fernandes

This talk was recorded at React Summit Amsterdam 2026: 
рџ”— https://reactsummit.com/?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=RosarioFernandes

рџ“Њ Follow the link to watch the full version of all the conference talks, QnAвЂ™s with speakers and hands-on workshop recordings:  
рџ”— https://gitnation.com/events/react-summit-2026?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=RosarioFernandes

рџ•ђ Timestamps:
00:00 - "I'm Actually an Android Dev" вЂ” RosГЎrio Takes the Stage
00:35 - Firebase Is a Client SDK. React Runs on the Server. Here's the Problem
01:18 - The Auth Flicker: Why Your App Shows a Login Button to Logged-In Users
01:57 - FirebaseServerApp: Fix Auth State Before the Page Even Loads
02:32 - Firestore Makes It Worse вЂ” Offline Cache + Real-Time = 3 Flickers Total
03:35 - toJSON() + onSnapshotResume: How to Serialize Your Way Out of the Flicker
04:39 - Remote Config on the Server: Feature Flags That Work Before JS Loads
05:16 - What's Next for Firebase on the Web (App Hosting, SQL Connect, Crashlytics)

snippet.title: One Rendering Layer for React, Angular & Vue. No Duplication - AG Grid Case Study | Stephen Cooper
snippet.description:
AG Grid supports React, Angular, Vue, and vanilla JS вЂ“ from one codebase, without duplicating logic for each framework. Here's the architecture experiment behind it рџ§Є

What's inside:
вљЎпёЏ Why complex component logic can't live in the view layer вЂ“ and where it should go instead
вљЎпёЏ How AG Grid stripped state, events, and tree structure out of framework components down to pure DOM primitives
вљЎпёЏ The abstraction layer that lets one component render natively in any framework вЂ“ and why performance still holds

рџЋ™ Stephen Cooper is Team Lead at AG Grid, 
where he's spent years deep in the codebase вЂ“ optimizing grid performance, solving framework integrations, and profiling more render cycles than he'd care to admit. He brings practical, experience-based insights straight from production.
